Thanks Skye!

As a group of 13 each buying drinks/food individually there needs to be a better way of ensuring people get their correct order. One person was given a “gin and tonic” which she assumed was the zero gin she had ordered - but it was alcoholic. Someone else got the zero gin before it was realised. I received the wrong food order as “sliders” were announced which I assumed were my steak sliders, but they were chicken and I ate them leaving the other person that doesn’t eat red meat without food. Simply adding a name to an order will combat these issues as just shouting the order when people are talking and all spread out didn’t work as we had no idea what each other had ordered so assumed it was for you. This could lead to serious allergy issues.
